words.definition: A long passage between rows of shelves in a store
words.example: The breakfast cereals are in aisle seven.

words.definition: The place in a store where customers pay for their purchases
words.example: There was a long queue at the self-service checkout.

words.definition: A voucher that entitles the holder to a discount on a product
words.example: I used a coupon to save two dollars on my breakfast cereal.

words.definition: A reduction from the normal selling price of a product
words.example: This store offers a ten percent discount for students.

words.definition: A printed document confirming that a payment has been made
words.example: Keep your receipt in case you need to return the item.

words.definition: A person whose job is to take payments at a store
words.example: The cashier scanned all my items and put them in a bag.

words.definition: The date after which a product should no longer be used or sold
words.example: Always check the expiration date before buying dairy products.

words.definition: Produced without the use of artificial chemicals or pesticides
words.example: Organic vegetables tend to cost more than conventionally grown ones.

words.definition: The amount or number of something that is available or required
words.example: Please enter the exact quantity you would like to order.

words.definition: A special offer that sells products at a reduced price for a limited time
words.example: The supermarket is running a buy-one-get-one promotion this week.

words.definition: A points programme that rewards customers for repeated purchases
words.example: Scan your loyalty card at checkout to earn points on every purchase.

words.definition: Buying a large quantity of something at once, usually at a lower price
words.example: It is much cheaper to buy rice and pasta in bulk.

words.definition: A product sold without a brand name, often cheaper than branded versions
words.example: Generic painkillers contain the same active ingredient as branded ones.

words.definition: Describing food or goods that will spoil or decay quickly
words.example: Keep all perishable items refrigerated at the correct temperature.

words.definition: The sale of goods in large quantities at low prices, usually to retailers
words.example: The restaurant buys its produce at wholesale prices to save money.

words.definition: A substance that triggers an allergic reaction, legally required to be declared on food labels
words.example: The packaging must list all fourteen major allergens in accordance with food labeling law.

words.definition: The ability to track the origin and complete journey of a product through the supply chain
words.example: Retailers must guarantee traceability from farm to shelf for all fresh produce.

words.definition: The amount added to the cost price of goods to cover expenses and generate profit
words.example: The supermarket's markup on premium organic lines is significantly higher than on standard products.

words.definition: The official withdrawal of a product from sale because of a safety or quality defect
words.example: The manufacturer issued a recall after a contamination risk was identified in the batch.

words.definition: A company or individual that provides goods to a retailer or business for resale or use
words.example: The supermarket works with over two hundred approved suppliers across multiple countries.

words.definition: The formal process by which a business sources and purchases goods or services from external suppliers
words.example: The procurement team negotiates long-term contracts to secure stable pricing from key suppliers.

words.definition: The uninterrupted temperature-controlled logistics system that preserves perishable goods from producer to consumer
words.example: A break in the cold chain during transport can compromise the safety of frozen or chilled products.

words.definition: The unexplained loss of retail inventory, typically due to theft, damage, or administrative error
words.example: The store introduced new security measures after shrinkage rates rose significantly last quarter.

words.definition: The entire network of producers, processors, distributors, and retailers involved in delivering a product to the consumer
words.example: Disruptions to the global supply chain caused widespread shortages and price increases.

words.definition: The illegal practice of adding inferior or harmful substances to food products, compromising their purity or safety
words.example: The food fraud investigation revealed widespread adulteration of imported honey with cheaper syrups.
